results.title: Amazon secures $17.5 billion loan facility amid AI-driven capex ramp - Reuters
results.content:
Skip to main content

# Amazon secures $17.5 billion loan facility amid AI-driven capex ramp

By Reuters

June 10 (Reuters) - Amazon.com (AMZN.O), opens new tab has secured a $17.5 billion loan from lenders ​including Citibank (C.N), opens new tab, the tech giant said, as ‌it ramps up spending on AI infrastructure.

The lenders also include BofA Securities (BAC.N), opens new tab, JPMorgan Chase (JPM.N), opens new tab, HSBC (HSBA.L), opens new tab and Wells Fargo (WFC.N), opens new tab, ​Amazon said in a filing dated June ​8, adding that the funding is for ⁠general corporate purposes.

The Reuters Inside Track newsletter is your essential guide during the World Cup. Sign up here. [...] Amazon's latest agreement is ​for a delayed draw term loan facility, ​which ⁠means it can withdraw amounts as needed instead of receiving the entire amount upfront.

Earlier this week, ⁠Amazon filed ​for a five-part debt offering ​in Canada for up to C$14 billion.

Reporting by Deborah Sophia and ​Anhata Rooprai in Bengaluru; Editing by Shilpi Majumdar

Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles., opens new tab

Here’s the commerce news, observations and tidbits that came across my desk (and into my DMs) this week. We’ll try to publish these roundups weekly from now on, so if there’s something you think should be included, email me.
